boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: Making your first API call on RingCentral

documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: Developer obtains valid app credentials, authenticates, and successfully calls a RingCentral API endpoint
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: API call returns a successful response indicating the call was placed or data retrieved
documented_first_success.official_milestone: Successfully making the first API call using the sample app with valid authentication
documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: The official Getting Started guide defines the first success as making a successful API call using the sample app after obtaining credentials and authenticating.

assumed_prior_state: Developer has a RingCentral account or can create one
Developer has basic knowledge of REST APIs and OAuth authentication
entry_point.developer_intent: To build an application or integration that uses RingCentral's communication APIs.

friction_gates.description: Developer must have or create a RingCentral account to register an app
App registration requires providing valid information to obtain client credentials
Developer must correctly implement OAuth or JWT authentication to obtain a valid token

prerequisites.requirement: Have a RingCentral account to register an application and obtain credentials
Register an application in the RingCentral Developer Portal to obtain client ID and client secret
Understand basic REST API concepts and OAuth authentication

primary_path.action: Navigate to the RingCentral Getting Started guide
details: Access the official documentation to begin the onboarding process
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Page fails to load or is inaccessible

primary_path.action: Register a new application in the RingCentral Developer Portal
details: Obtain client ID and client secret credentials
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Registration errors or missing required info

primary_path.action: Generate an authentication token using the app credentials
details: Use OAuth or JWT authentication methods as per documentation
primary_path.input: Client ID, client secret, and authentication method
primary_path.success_signal: Token valid and usable for API calls

primary_path.action: Make a sample API call using the access token
details: Call an endpoint such as placing a RingOut call or retrieving call logs
primary_path.input: API request with access token and parameters
primary_path.output: API response with call status or data
primary_path.success_signal: API returns successful response indicating call placed or data retrieved

sections_used: Embed a phone and log calls seamlessly in your CRM
sources.title: RingEX Integration Apps | RingCentral Developers

sections_used: Sample code for making API calls
sources.title: RingCentral Developers
Making your first API call on RingCentral
Methods, endpoints and parameters of the RingCentral API

alternatives_considered.name: SDKs
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: SDKs are client libraries to facilitate API usage but the core first-mile onboarding is documented via the REST API usage.

alternatives_considered.name: Embeddable Communications Client (RingEX)
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: RingEX is a specialized integration solution for embedding communications in CRM apps, not the primary onboarding surface for general API developers.

surface.name: REST API
surface.selection_basis: The RingCentral platform primarily exposes its developer capabilities through a REST API, as documented in the official RingCentral Developer Guide and API Reference.

uncertainties.impact: Without a vendor claim or documented metric, time estimates for onboarding cannot be provided.
uncertainties.question: What is the typical or average time to first success as claimed by RingCentral?
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: Official documentation does not specify any time-to-first-success metric or estimate.
